title: Part 1: Consistent Hashing — a ring so adding a server moves few keys

cues.label: Modulo fails
cues.headline: Change N, reshuffle all
cues.narration: Server equals hash modulo N reshuffles nearly every key when N changes — a cache-miss storm.

cues.label: The ring
cues.headline: First server clockwise owns it
cues.narration: Put servers and keys on a ring; a key is owned by the first server clockwise from its point.

cues.label: Add a server
cues.headline: Only one arc moves
cues.narration: A new server drops one point and only steals the keys in its arc — about one-Nth of them.

cues.label: Virtual nodes
cues.headline: Many replicas smooth load
cues.narration: One point per server gives uneven arcs; many replicas per server average to a fair share.

cues.label: Fast lookup
cues.headline: Binary search the ring
cues.narration: Keep the positions sorted and binary-search for the next one clockwise — order log n per lookup.

cues.label: Remove a server
cues.headline: Its arc spills to a neighbor
cues.narration: Removing a server hands its arc to the next neighbor clockwise; nothing else moves.
